contentBlocks.original:
न क्रोधो न च मात्सर्यं न लोभो नाशुभामतिः ।
भवन्ति कृतपुण्यानां भक्तानां पुरुषोत्तमे ॥१३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na krōdhō na cha mātsaryaṃ na lōbhō nāśubhāmatiḥ ।
bhavanti kṛtapuṇyānāṃ bhaktānāṃ puruṣōttamē ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The devotee of the Lord Purushottama, has neither anger nor fear, nor avarice and nor bad thoughts.
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; क्रोधः (krodhaḥ) – anger; न (na) – not; च (ca) – and; मात्सर्यम् (mātsaryam) – envy; न (na) – not; लोभः (lobhaḥ) – greed; न (na) – not; अशुभा (aśubhā) – inauspicious; मतिः (matiḥ) – thought; भवन्ति (bhavanti) – become; कृतपुण्यानाम् (kṛtapuṇyānām) – of those with good deeds (kṛta:done | puṇyānām:of the virtuous); भक्तानाम् (bhaktānām) – of the devotees; पुरुषोत्तमे (puruṣottame) – in the Supreme Person (puruṣa:person | uttame:supreme);

contentBlocks.original:
द्यौः स-चन्द्रार्क नक्षत्रा खं दिशो भूर्महोदधिः ।
वासुदेवस्य वीर्येण विधृतानि महात्मनः ॥१४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dyauḥ sa-chandrārka nakṣatrā khaṃ diśō bhūrmahōdadhiḥ ।
vāsudēvasya vīryēṇa vidhṛtāni mahātmanaḥ ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The heavens, the moon, the sun, the stars, the sky, the directions, the earth and the ocean are sustained by the might of the great soul Vasudeva.
contentBlocks.meanings: द्यौः (dyauḥ) – the heavens; स-चन्द्रार्क (sa-candrārka) – with moon and sun (sa:with | candra:moon | arka:sun); नक्षत्रा (nakṣatrā) – the stars; खं (khaṃ) – the sky; दिशः (diśaḥ) – the directions; भूः (bhūḥ) – the earth; महोदधिः (mahōdadhiḥ) – the great ocean (maha:great | udadhi:ocean); वासुदेवस्य (vāsudēvasya) – of Vasudeva; वीर्येण (vīryēṇa) – by the might; विधृतानि (vidhṛtāni) – are sustained; महात्मनः (mahātmanaḥ) – of the great soul (mahā:great | ātmanaḥ:soul);

contentBlocks.original:
ससुरासुरगन्धर्वं सयक्षोरगराक्षसम् ।
जगद्वशे वर्ततेदं कृष्णस्य स चराचरम्। ॥१५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sasurāsuragandharvaṃ sayakṣōragarākṣasam ।
jagadvaśē vartatēdaṃ kṛṣṇasya sa charācharam। ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: All this world, that which moves and moves not, and which has Devas, Rakshasas and Gandharwas, and also Asuras and Nagas, are under the control of Lord Krishna.
contentBlocks.meanings: ससुरासुरगन्धर्वं (sasurāsuragandharvaṃ) – with Devas, Asuras, Gandharvas (sa:with | surā:Devas | asura:Asuras | gandharvaṃ:Gandharvas); सयक्षोरगराक्षसम् (sayakṣoragarākṣasam) – with Yakshas, Nagas, Rakshasas (sa:with | yakṣa:Yakshas | uraga:Nagas | rākṣasam:Rakshasas); जगद्वशे (jagadvaśe) – under the control of world (jagat:world | vaśe:under control); वर्तते (vartate) – exists; इदं (idaṃ) – this; कृष्णस्य (kṛṣṇasya) – of Krishna; स (sa) – with; चराचरम् (carācaram) – moving and non-moving (cara:moving | acaram:non-moving);

contentBlocks.original:
इन्द्रियाणि मनोबुद्धिः सत्त्वं तेजो बलं धृतिः ।
वासुदेवात्मकान्याहुः, क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञ एव च ॥१६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
indriyāṇi manōbuddhiḥ sattvaṃ tējō balaṃ dhṛtiḥ ।
vāsudēvātmakānyāhuḥ, kṣētraṃ kṣētrajña ēva cha ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The senses, mind, intellect, Sattva, splendour, strength and patience are said to be composed of Vasudeva, the field and the knower of the field.
contentBlocks.meanings: इन्द्रियाणि (indriyāṇi) – senses; मनोबुद्धिः (manobuddhiḥ) – mind and intellect (manaḥ:mind | buddhiḥ:intellect); सत्त्वं (sattvaṃ) – Sattva; तेजः (tejaḥ) – splendour; बलं (balaṃ) – strength; धृतिः (dhṛtiḥ) – patience; वासुदेवात्मकानि (vāsudevātmakāni) – composed of Vasudeva (vāsudeva:Vasudeva | ātmakāni:composed of); आहुः (āhuḥ) – are said; क्षेत्रं (kṣetraṃ) – field; क्षेत्रज्ञ (kṣetrajña) – knower of the field; एव (eva) – indeed; च (ca) – and;

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वागमानामाचारः प्रथमं परिकल्पते ।
आचारप्रभवो धर्मो धर्मस्य प्रभुरच्युतः ॥१७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvāgamānāmāchāraḥ prathamaṃ parikalpatē ।
āchāraprabhavō dharmō dharmasya prabhurachyutaḥ ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The conduct of all the Vedas is first conceived as the origin of conduct, the Dharma, the Lord of Dharma, the infallible.
contentBlocks.meanings: सर्वागमानाम् (sarvāgamānām) – of all scriptures (sarva:all | āgamānām:of scriptures); आचारः (ācāraḥ) – conduct; प्रथमं (prathamaṃ) – first; परिकल्पते (parikalpate) – is conceived; आचारप्रभवः (ācāraprabhavaḥ) – origin of conduct (ācāra:conduct | prabhavaḥ:origin); धर्मः (dharmaḥ) – dharma; धर्मस्य (dharmasya) – of dharma; प्रभुः (prabhuḥ) – lord; अच्युतः (acyutaḥ) – infallible;

contentBlocks.original:
ऋषयः पितरो देवा महाभूतानि धातवः ।
जङ्गमाजङ्गमं चेदं जगन्नारायणोद्भवम् ॥१८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ṛṣayaḥ pitarō dēvā mahābhūtāni dhātavaḥ ।
jaṅgamājaṅgamaṃ chēdaṃ jagannārāyaṇōdbhavam ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The sages, the forefathers, the gods, the great beings, the metals, the movable and the immovable, this universe is born of Narayana.
contentBlocks.meanings: ऋषयः (ṛṣayaḥ) – sages; पितरः (pitarāḥ) – forefathers; देवाः (devāḥ) – gods; महाभूतानि (mahābhūtāni) – great beings (mahā:great | bhūtāni:beings); धातवः (dhātavaḥ) – metals; जङ्गम (jaṅgama) – movable; अजङ्गमम् (ajaṅgamam) – immovable; च (ca) – and; इदम् (idam) – this; जगत् (jagat) – universe; नारायणोद्भवम् (nārāyaṇodbhavam) – born of Narayana (nārāyaṇa:Narayana | udbhavam:born of);

contentBlocks.original:
योगोज्ञानं तथा साङ्ख्यं विद्याः शिल्पादिकर्म च ।
वेदाः शास्त्राणि विज्ञानमेतत्सर्वं जनार्दनात् ॥१९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yōgōjñānaṃ tathā sāṅkhyaṃ vidyāḥ śilpādikarma cha ।
vēdāḥ śāstrāṇi vijñānamētatsarvaṃ janārdanāt ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Yoga, knowledge and also Sāṅkhya, the sciences, crafts and other actions, the Vedas, the scriptures and knowledge, all this comes from Janardana.
contentBlocks.meanings: योग (yoga) – yoga; ज्ञानं (jñānaṃ) – knowledge; तथा (tathā) – and also; साङ्ख्यं (sāṅkhyaṃ) – Sāṅkhya; विद्याः (vidyāḥ) – sciences; शिल्पादिकर्म (śilpādikarma) – crafts and other actions (śilpa:crafts | ādi:and others | karma:actions); च (ca) – and; वेदाः (vedāḥ) – the Vedas; शास्त्राणि (śāstrāṇi) – the scriptures; विज्ञानम् (vijñānam) – knowledge; एतत्सर्वं (etatsarvaṃ) – all this (etat:this | sarvaṃ:all); जनार्दनात् (janārdanāt) – from Janardana;

contentBlocks.original:
एको विष्णुर्महद्भूतं पृथग्भूतान्यनेकशः ।
त्रींलोकान्व्याप्य भूतात्मा भुङ्क्ते विश्वभुगव्ययः ॥२०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ēkō viṣṇurmahadbhūtaṃ pṛthagbhūtānyanēkaśaḥ ।
trīṃlōkānvyāpya bhūtātmā bhuṅktē viśvabhugavyayaḥ ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Vishnu alone, the Great Being, pervading the three worlds with many separate beings, the Soul of beings, enjoys the expanse of the Enjoyer of the universe.
contentBlocks.meanings: एकः (ekaḥ) – one; विष्णुः (viṣṇuḥ) – Vishnu; महद्भूतम् (mahadbhūtam) – the Great Being (mahat:great | bhūtam:being); पृथक् (pṛthak) – separate; भूतानि (bhūtāni) – beings; अनेकशः (anekaśaḥ) – in many ways; त्रीन् (trīn) – three; लोकान् (lokān) – worlds; व्याप्य (vyāpya) – pervading; भूतात्मा (bhūtātmā) – Soul of beings (bhūta:beings | ātmā:soul); भुङ्क्ते (bhuṅkte) – enjoys; विश्वभुक् (viśvabhuk) – Enjoyer of the universe (viśva:universe | bhuk:enjoyer); अव्ययः (avyayaḥ) – imperishable;

contentBlocks.original:
इमं स्तवं भगवतो विष्णोर्व्यासेन कीर्तितम् ।
पठेद्य इच्चेत्पुरुषः श्रेयः प्राप्तुं सुखानि च ॥२१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
imaṃ stavaṃ bhagavatō viṣṇōrvyāsēna kīrtitam ।
paṭhēdya ichchētpuruṣaḥ śrēyaḥ prāptuṃ sukhāni cha ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Any man who desires to attain prosperity and happiness should recite this hymn of Lord Vishnu recited by Vyasa.
contentBlocks.meanings: इमं (imaṃ) – this; स्तवम् (stavam) – hymn; भगवतो (bhagavato) – of the Lord; विष्णोः (viṣṇoḥ) – of Vishnu; व्यासेन (vyāsena) – by Vyasa; कीर्तितम् (kīrtitam) – recited; पठेत् (paṭhet) – should recite; यः (yaḥ) – who; इच्चेत् (icchet) – desires; पुरुषः (puruṣaḥ) – man; श्रेयः (śreyaḥ) – prosperity; प्राप्तुम् (prāptum) – to attain; सुखानि (sukhāni) – happiness; च (ca) – and;

contentBlocks.original:
विश्वेश्वरमजं देवं जगतः प्रभुमव्ययम्।
भजन्ति ये पुष्कराक्षं न ते यान्ति पराभवम् ॥२२॥
न ते यान्ति पराभवं ॐ नम इति ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśvēśvaramajaṃ dēvaṃ jagataḥ prabhumavyayam।
bhajanti yē puṣkarākṣaṃ na tē yānti parābhavam ॥ 22 ॥
na tē yānti parābhavaṃ ōṃ nama iti ।
contentBlocks.translation: Those who worship the lotus-eyed Lord of the universe, the unborn God, the Lord of the universe, the inexhaustible, do not get defeated.
contentBlocks.meanings: विश्वेश्वरम् (viśveśvaram) – Lord of the universe (viśva:universe | īśvaram:Lord); अजम् (ajam) – unborn; देवम् (devam) – God; जगतः (jagataḥ) – of the universe; प्रभुम् (prabhum) – Lord; अव्ययम् (avyayam) – inexhaustible; भजन्ति (bhajanti) – worship; ये (ye) – those who; पुष्कराक्षम् (puṣkarākṣam) – lotus-eyed (puṣkara:lotus | akṣam:eye); न (na) – not; ते (te) – they; यान्ति (yānti) – go; पराभवम् (parābhavam) – defeat;

contentBlocks.original:
अर्जुन उवाच
पद्मपत्र विशालाक्ष पद्मनाभ सुरोत्तम ।
भक्ताना मनुरक्तानां त्राता भव जनार्दन ॥२३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
arjuna uvācha
padmapatra viśālākṣa padmanābha surōttama ।
bhaktānā manuraktānāṃ trātā bhava janārdana ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Arjuna said — O lotus-petalled, large-eyed, lotus-naveled, best of the gods, be the savior of the devotees who are devoted to you, O Janardana.
contentBlocks.meanings: अर्जुन (arjuna) – Arjuna; उवाच (uvāca) – said; पद्मपत्र (padmapatra) – lotus-petalled (padma:lotus | patra:petal); विशालाक्ष (viśālākṣa) – large-eyed (viśāla:large | akṣa:eye); पद्मनाभ (padmanābha) – lotus-naveled (padma:lotus | nābha:navel); सुरोत्तम (surottama) – best of the gods (sura:god | uttama:best); भक्तानाम् (bhaktānām) – of the devotees; अनुरक्तानाम् (anuraktānām) – devoted to you; त्राता (trātā) – savior; भव (bhava) – be; जनार्दन (janārdana) – Janardana;

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीभगवानुवाच
यो मां नामसहस्रेण स्तोतुमिच्छति पाण्डव ।
सोऽहमेकेन श्लोकेन स्तुत एव न संशयः ॥२४॥
स्तुत एव न संशय ॐ नम इति ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrībhagavānuvācha
yō māṃ nāmasahasrēṇa stōtumichChati pāṇḍava ।
sō'hamēkēna ślōkēna stuta ēva na saṃśayaḥ ॥ 24 ॥
stuta ēva na saṃśaya ōṃ nama iti ।
contentBlocks.translation: The Lord said: He who likes, Oh Arjuna, to sing my praise, using these thousand names, should know Arjuna, that I would be satisfied By his singing of even one stanza, without any doubt.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्रीभगवानुवाच (śrībhagavānuvāca) – the Lord said (śrī:auspicious | bhagavān:the Lord | uvāca:said); यः (yaḥ) – who; माम् (mām) – me; नामसहस्रेण (nāmasahasreṇa) – with a thousand names (nāma:name | sahasreṇa:with a thousand); स्तोतुम् (stotum) – to praise; इच्छति (icchati) – desires; पाण्डव (pāṇḍava) – O son of Pandu; सः (saḥ) – he; अहम् (aham) – I; एकेन (ekena) – with one; श्लोकेन (ślokena) – with a verse; स्तुतः (stutaḥ) – praised; एव (eva) – indeed; न (na) – not; संशयः (saṃśayaḥ) – doubt;

contentBlocks.original:
व्यास उवाच
वासनाद्वासुदेवस्य वासितं भुवनत्रयम् ।
सर्वभूतनिवासोऽसि वासुदेव नमोऽस्तु ते ॥२५॥
श्रीवासुदेव नमोस्तुत ॐ नम इति ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vyāsa uvācha
vāsanādvāsudēvasya vāsitaṃ bhuvanatrayam ।
sarvabhūtanivāsō'si vāsudēva namō'stu tē ॥ 25 ॥
śrīvāsudēva namōstuta ōṃ nama iti ।
contentBlocks.translation: Vyasa said: My salutations to you Vasudeva, because you who live in all the worlds make these worlds as places where beings live, and also Vasudeva, You live in all beings as their soul. Om Nama salutations to Vasudeva.
contentBlocks.meanings: व्यास (vyāsa) – Vyasa; उवाच (uvāca) – said; वासनात् (vāsanāt) – due to fragrance; वासुदेवस्य (vāsudevasya) – of Vasudeva; वासितं (vāsitaṃ) – perfumed; भुवनत्रयम् (bhuvanatrayam) – three worlds (bhuvana:world | trayam:three); सर्वभूतनिवासः (sarvabhūtanivāsaḥ) – abode of all beings (sarva:all | bhūta:beings | nivāsaḥ:abode); असि (asi) – you are; वासुदेव (vāsudeva) – Vasudeva; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; अस्तु (astu) – let there be; ते (te) – to you;

contentBlocks.original:
पार्वत्युवाच
केनोपायेन लघुना विष्णोर्नामसहस्रकम् ।
पठ्यते पण्डितैर्नित्यं श्रोतुमिच्छाम्यहं प्रभो ॥२६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pārvatyuvācha
kēnōpāyēna laghunā viṣṇōrnāmasahasrakam ।
paṭhyatē paṇḍitairnityaṃ śrōtumichChāmyahaṃ prabhō ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Parvathi said: I am desirous to know, Oh Lord, how the scholars of this world will chant without fail these thousand names, by a method that is easy and quick.
contentBlocks.meanings: पार्वत्युवाच (pārvatyuvāca) – Parvati said (pārvatī:Parvati | uvāca:said); केन (kena) – by what; उपायेन (upāyena) – method; लघुना (laghunā) – easy and quick; विष्णोः (viṣṇoḥ) – of Vishnu; नामसहस्रकम् (nāmasahasrakam) – thousand names (nāma:names | sahasrakam:thousand); पठ्यते (paṭhyate) – is chanted; पण्डितैः (paṇḍitaiḥ) – by scholars; नित्यम् (nityam) – always; श्रोतुम् (śrotum) – to hear; इच्छामि (icchāmi) – I desire; अहम् (aham) – I; प्रभो (prabho) – Oh Lord;

contentBlocks.original:
ईश्वर उवाच
श्रीराम राम रामेति रमे रामे मनोरमे ।
सहस्रनाम तत्तुल्यं रामनाम वरानने ॥२७॥
श्रीराम नाम वरानन ॐ नम इति ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
īśvara uvācha
(Chant this shloka 3 times)
śrīrāma rāma rāmēti ramē rāmē manōramē ।
sahasranāma tattulyaṃ rāmanāma varānanē ॥ 27 ॥
śrīrāma nāma varānana ōṃ nama iti ।
contentBlocks.translation: O beautiful-faced one, I delight in the name ‘Rāma, Rāma, Rāma’; the name of Rāma is equal to the thousand names (of the Lord).
contentBlocks.meanings: ईश्वर (īśvara) – Lord; उवाच (uvāca) – said; श्रीराम (śrīrāma) – glorious Rāma (śrī:glorious | rāma:Rāma); राम (rāma) – Rāma; रामेति (rāmeti) – Rāma, thus (rāma:Rāma | iti:thus); रमे (rame) – I delight; रामे (rāme) – in Rāma; मनोरमे (manorame) – O beautiful-faced one; सहस्रनाम (sahasranāma) – thousand names (sahasra:thousand | nāma:names); तत्तुल्यं (tattulyaṃ) – equal to that (tat:that | tulyaṃ:equal); रामनाम (rāmanāma) – name of Rāma (rāma:Rāma | nāma:name); वरानने (varānane) – O beautiful-faced one;

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मोवाच
नमोऽस्त्वनन्ताय सहस्रमूर्तये सहस्रपादाक्षिशिरोरुबाहवे ।
सहस्रनाम्ने पुरुषाय शाश्वते सहस्रकोटी युगधारिणे नमः ॥२८॥
श्री सहस्रकोटी युगधारिणे नम ॐ नम इति ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmōvācha
namō'stvanantāya sahasramūrtayē sahasrapādākṣiśirōrubāhavē ।
sahasranāmnē puruṣāya śāśvatē sahasrakōṭī yugadhāriṇē namaḥ ॥ 28 ॥
śrī sahasrakōṭī yugadhāriṇē nama ōṃ nama iti ।
contentBlocks.translation: Brahma said: Salutations to Thee, Oh lord, Who runs the immeasurable time of thousands of crore yugas, Who has no end, Who has a thousand names, Who has a thousand forms, Who has a thousand feet, Who has a thousand eyes, Who has a thousand heads, Who has a thousand arms, and Who is always there. Om Nama He who runs thousands of crore yugas.
contentBlocks.meanings: ब्रह्मोवाच (brahmovāca) – Brahma said (brahma:Brahma | uvāca:said); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; अस्तु (astu) – be; अनन्ताय (anantāya) – to the endless one; सहस्रमूर्तये (sahasramūrtaye) – to the thousand-formed (sahasra:thousand | mūrtaye:to the formed); सहस्रपादाक्षिशिरोरुबाहवे (sahasrapādākṣiśirorubāhave) – with thousand feet, eyes, heads, arms (sahasra:thousand | pāda:feet | akṣi:eyes | śiraḥ:heads | ūru:thighs | bāhave:arms); सहस्रनाम्ने (sahasranāmne) – to the thousand-named (sahasra:thousand | nāmne:to the named); पुरुषाय (puruṣāya) – to the person; शाश्वते (śāśvate) – eternal; सहस्रकोटी (sahasrakōṭī) – thousands of crores (sahasra:thousand | koṭī:crores); युगधारिणे (yugadhāriṇe) – who sustains the yugas (yuga:yugas | dhāriṇe:who sustains); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations;

contentBlocks.original:
सञ्जय उवाच
यत्र योगेश्वरः कृष्णो यत्र पार्थो धनुर्धरः ।
तत्र श्रीर्विजयो भूतिर्ध्रुवा नीतिर्मतिर्मम ॥२९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sañjaya uvācha
yatra yōgēśvaraḥ kṛṣṇō yatra pārthō dhanurdharaḥ ।
tatra śrīrvijayō bhūtirdhruvā nītirmatirmama ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sanjaya said: Where Krishna, the king of Yogas, and where the wielder of bow, Arjuna, is there, there will exist all the good, all the the victory, all the fame, and all the justice in this world.
contentBlocks.meanings: सञ्जय (sañjaya) – Sanjaya; उवाच (uvāca) – said; यत्र (yatra) – where; योगेश्वरः (yogeśvaraḥ) – lord of yoga (yoga:yoga | īśvaraḥ:lord); कृष्णः (kṛṣṇaḥ) – Krishna; यत्र (yatra) – where; पार्थः (pārthaḥ) – son of Pritha (Arjuna); धनुर्धरः (dhanurdharaḥ) – wielder of the bow (dhanus:bow | dharaḥ:holder); तत्र (tatra) – there; श्रीः (śrīḥ) – prosperity; विजयः (vijayaḥ) – victory; भूतिः (bhūtiḥ) – prosperity; ध्रुवा (dhruvā) – certain; नीतिः (nītiḥ) – justice; मतिः (matiḥ) – opinion; मम (mama) – my;

contentBlocks.original:
श्री भगवान् उवाच
अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते ।
तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम्। ॥३०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrī bhagavān uvācha
ananyāśchintayantō māṃ yē janāḥ paryupāsatē ।
tēṣāṃ nityābhiyuktānāṃ yōgakṣēmaṃ vahāmyaham। ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sri Bhagavan said: I would take care of worries and cares of him who thinks and serves Me without any other thoughts.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्री (śrī) – auspicious; भगवान् (bhagavān) – the Lord; उवाच (uvāca) – said; अनन्याः (ananyāḥ) – undistracted; चिन्तयन्तः (cintayantaḥ) – thinking; माम् (mām) – me; ये (ye) – who; जनाः (janāḥ) – people; पर्युपासते (paryupāsate) – worship; तेषाम् (teṣām) – of them; नित्याभियुक्तानाम् (nityābhiyuktānām) – constantly engaged (nitya:constant | abhiyuktānām:engaged); योगक्षेमम् (yogakṣemam) – welfare and security (yoga:welfare | kṣema:security); वहामि (vahāmi) – carry; अहम् (aham) – I;

contentBlocks.original:
परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम्। ।
धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय सम्भवामि युगे युगे ॥३१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṃ vināśāya cha duṣkṛtām। ।
dharmasaṃsthāpanārthāya sambhavāmi yugē yugē ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I save the righteous and destroy the wicked, and establish righteousness in every age.
contentBlocks.meanings: परित्राणाय (paritrāṇāya) – for the protection; साधूनां (sādhūnāṃ) – of the righteous; विनाशाय (vināśāya) – for the destruction; च (ca) – and; दुष्कृताम् (duṣkṛtām) – of the wicked; धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय (dharmasaṃsthāpanārthāya) – for establishing righteousness (dharma:righteousness | saṃsthāpana:establishing | arthāya:for the purpose of); सम्भवामि (sambhavāmi) – I manifest; युगे (yuge) – in age; युगे (yuge) – in age;

contentBlocks.original:
आर्ताः विषण्णाः शिथिलाश्च भीताः घोरेषु च व्याधिषु वर्तमानाः ।
सङ्कीर्त्य नारायणशब्दमात्रं विमुक्तदुःखाः सुखिनो भवन्ति ॥३२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ārtāḥ viṣaṇṇāḥ śithilāścha bhītāḥ ghōrēṣu cha vyādhiṣu vartamānāḥ ।
saṅkīrtya nārāyaṇaśabdamātraṃ vimuktaduḥkhāḥ sukhinō bhavanti ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: If he who is worried, sad, broken, afraid, severely ill, if he who has heard tidings bad, sings Narayana and Narayana, all his cares would be taken care of.
contentBlocks.meanings: आर्ताः (ārtāḥ) – worried; विषण्णाः (viṣaṇṇāḥ) – sad; शिथिलाः (śithilāḥ) – broken; च (ca) – and; भीताः (bhītāḥ) – afraid; घोरेषु (ghoreṣu) – in severe; च (ca) – and; व्याधिषु (vyādhiṣu) – illnesses; वर्तमानाः (vartamānāḥ) – being present; सङ्कीर्त्य (saṅkīrtya) – singing; नारायणशब्दमात्रं (nārāyaṇaśabdamātraṃ) – just the word Narayana (nārāyaṇa:Narayana | śabda:word | mātraṃ:just); विमुक्तदुःखाः (vimuktaduḥkhāḥ) – freed from sorrow (vimukta:freed | duḥkhāḥ:sorrow); सुखिनः (sukhinaḥ) – happy; भवन्ति (bhavanti) – become;

contentBlocks.original:
कायेन वाचा मनसेन्द्रियैर्वा बुद्ध्यात्मना वा प्रकृतेः स्वभावात् ।
करोमि यद्यत्सकलं परस्मै नारायणायेति समर्पयामि ॥३३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāyēna vāchā manasēndriyairvā buddhyātmanā vā prakṛtēḥ svabhāvāt ।
karōmi yadyatsakalaṃ parasmai nārāyaṇāyēti samarpayāmi ॥ 33 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whatever I do either by body, speech, mind or sensory organs, either with my personal knowledge or natural trait, I surrender and submit all to that to supreme divine Narayana.
contentBlocks.meanings: कायेन (kāyena) – by body; वाचा (vācā) – by speech; मनसा (manasā) – by mind; इन्द्रियैः (indriyaiḥ) – by sensory organs; वा (vā) – or; बुद्ध्या (buddhyā) – by intellect; आत्मना (ātmanā) – by self; वा (vā) – or; प्रकृतेः (prakṛteḥ) – of nature; स्वभावात् (svabhāvāt) – by natural trait; करोमि (karomi) – I do; यत् (yat) – whatever; यत् (yat) – whatever; सकलं (sakalaṃ) – all; परस्मै (parasmai) – to the supreme; नारायणाय (nārāyaṇāya) – to Narayana; इति (iti) – thus; समर्पयामि (samarpayāmi) – I surrender;
